title: Under-Fortress Entry: The Throat of Stone
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, descending stone throat tunnel, wet walls, lantern in darkness, cinematic, no text
description:
The air changes.

Colder.

Older.

The walls feel shaped by hands that hated daylight.

Ahead: faint torchlight, and the sound of distant iron on stone.

choices.label: Follow the torchlight toward voices (possible market).
Take the narrower route toward a guardpost (riskier, faster).

title: Goblin Market: Quiet Violence
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, underground market with goblins trading, hanging lanterns, strange coins, guarded stalls, cinematic, no text
description:
A market in the dark.

Not friendly.

Just functional.

You can buy information with undercoin, trade favors, or start a fight that turns the whole cavern against you.

choices.label: Trade for Bonewhite Undercoin (requires a city coin sacrifice).
Investigate whispers of ‘a princess’ and ‘a crystal key’.

title: Guardpost: Iron and Habit
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, underground guardpost with iron bars and torchlight, disciplined captain silhouette, cinematic, no text
description:
A disciplined captain holds this post.

Not a goblin.

A hobgoblin trying to make retreat look like strategy.

Without a banner, he uses fear instead.

title: Choice: What Ends the War Faster
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, underground crossroads with two carved doorways, rune-lit paths, cinematic, no text
description:
Two paths are offered by rumor and necessity:

1) Prisoner Vault — captives (and a princess) used as leverage.
2) Crystal Nexus — the enemy’s war-sight, their hidden advantage.

Both matter.

But you can’t do everything before they adapt.

choices.label: 2) Go to the Crystal Nexus (seize war-sight).

title: Prisoner Vault: The Caged Promise
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, underground prison corridor, iron doors, muffled cries, lantern reflection, cinematic, no text
description:
Iron doors. Old locks.

You smell fear.

If you promised to look for captives, this is where promises become real—or become shame.

choices.label: Attempt to open the cell block (mind check).

title: Prison Choice: Save Many or Save One
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, prison junction with two doors, one ornate, one crowded with hands through bars, cinematic, no text
description:
Two doors:

- The crowded cells (many captives).
- The ornate cell (a high-value prisoner — ‘princess’ by rumor).

Time is a weapon.

Choose.

choices.label: 1) Free the many (city morale, future allies).

title: Crystal Nexus: The Enemy’s Sight
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, crystal-lined tunnel, pale glow, runes, cold mist, cinematic, no text
description:
Crystal threads the stone.

You feel it in your teeth.

If the enemy has war-sight, they will always strike first.

Unless you take it.

title: Nexus Chamber: Turn the Tide
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, large crystal chamber with sealed dais, keyhole glowing, reflections like stars, cinematic, no text
description:
The chamber hums.

This is not treasure.

This is advantage.

You can claim it, shatter it, or re-tune it to feed the city instead.

choices.label: 3) Re-tune it for the city (mind check).

title: Return Route: Veteran Steps
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, climbing back through tunnels toward faint daylight, lantern low, cinematic, no text
description:
You return with something the enemy didn’t expect:

- freed captives,
- political leverage,
- or a stolen advantage made of crystal.

You are no longer a novice.

You are a factor.

choices.label: Begin the next chapter: Mountain Fortress and the War’s True Heart.
